top_signals.title: Japan stablecoin payments advance with Lawson trial, Netstars launch
top_signals.summary: Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo as Netstars launches a merchant service supporting USDC, USDT and JPYC.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Roadmap for collaboration UK-Spain
top_signals.summary: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves and First Vice-President and Minister of Economy, Trade and Business Carlos Cuerpo Caballero have signed a roadmap on UK-Spain collaboration in Brussels on 9 July 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: As Milei’s Aura Fades, Argentina Starts to Look for a Third Way
top_signals.summary: Corruption was once Javier Milei’s strongest card against the Peronist movement that dominated Argentine politics for decades. Now it’s just one of several vulnerabilities that are damaging the libertarian president’s brand and giving wings to the opposition’s comeback plans.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: S&P Affirms Indonesia’s Investment-Grade Rating, Keeps Stable Outlook
top_signals.summary: S&P Global Ratings affirmed Indonesia’s investment-grade score and stable outlook, offering reassurance to investors and bucking a trend among peers that have turned more cautious on Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

top_signals.title: Commission approves Baker Hughes' acquisition of Chart Industries, subject to conditions
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 10 Jul 2026 The European Commission has approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Baker Hughes Company (‘Baker Hughes') of Chart Industries, Inc. (‘Chart'), both headquartered in the US. The approval is conditional upon full compliance with the commitments offered by Baker Hughes and Chart.
